description: New papers added to back the 5 stubfill notes (mixed-precision-and-stability, adaptation-and-merging, sft, rlaif-and-constitutional, structured-output) drafted on 2026-05-04. Per the Phase 2 subagent playbook, this file is merged into papers.json by the orchestrator. New excerpt files written for the heavily-cited subset.

new_papers.title: Mixed Precision Training
new_papers.authors: Micikevicius, Narang, Alben, Diamos, Elsen, Garcia, Ginsburg, Houston, Kuchaiev, Venkatesh, Wu
new_papers.summary: Canonical mixed-precision recipe: FP16 forward/backward + FP32 master weights + dynamic loss scaling + FP32 GEMM accumulators. The substrate that all modern lower-precision training extends.

new_papers.title: A Study of BFLOAT16 for Deep Learning Training
new_papers.summary: Establishes BF16 (8-bit exponent, 7-bit mantissa) as a drop-in FP32 replacement for deep-learning training, eliminating the need for loss scaling and (often) FP32 master weights. The universal LLM pre-training default by 2023.

new_papers.title: Megatron-LM: Training Multi-Billion Parameter Language Models Using Model Parallelism
new_papers.authors: Shoeybi, Patwary, Puri, LeGresley, Casper, Catanzaro
new_papers.summary: Original Megatron-LM tensor-parallelism paper. Cited here for the mixed-precision communication-precision discussion.

new_papers.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
new_papers.authors: Hu, Shen, Wallis, Allen-Zhu, Li, Wang, Wang, Chen
new_papers.summary: Canonical low-rank parameter-efficient fine-tuning. Inject trainable B*A rank-r decomposition alongside frozen base weights; merge for inference.

new_papers.title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs
new_papers.summary: LoRA over a 4-bit-quantized (NF4) base, with double quantization and paged optimizers. Lets a 65B parameter model fine-tune on a single 48GB GPU.

new_papers.title: Model soups: averaging weights of multiple fine-tuned models improves accuracy without increasing inference time
new_papers.authors: Wortsman, Ilharco, Gadre, Roelofs, Gontijo-Lopes, Morcos, Namkoong, Farhadi, Carmon, Kornblith, Schmidt
new_papers.summary: Averaging weights of multiple fine-tunes from a shared base produces a single model that often beats the best individual fine-tune. Foundational to the merging literature; relies on linear mode connectivity.

new_papers.title: Editing Models with Task Arithmetic
new_papers.authors: Ilharco, Ribeiro, Wortsman, Gururangan, Schmidt, Hajishirzi, Farhadi
new_papers.summary: Defines task vectors as fine-tune-minus-base directions in weight space; addition / negation / combination as primitives. Direct ancestor of TIES-Merging.

new_papers.title: TIES-Merging: Resolving Interference When Merging Models
new_papers.summary: Three-step model merge (Trim, Elect-Sign, Disjoint-Merge) addressing the sign-conflict and redundancy issues in naive task-vector averaging. The current default merge method in MergeKit.

new_papers.title: Efficient Guided Generation for Large Language Models
new_papers.summary: Reformulates constrained decoding as transitions in an FSM whose alphabet is the model's vocabulary. Builds a per-state index over allowed tokens, dropping per-step overhead from O(V) to amortized O(1). Foundation for Outlines, XGrammar, llguidance.

new_papers.title: Prompting Is Programming: A Query Language for Large Language Models
new_papers.summary: Introduces LMQL, a query language for LLMs that exposes constraints (regex, length, type) close to the prompt and translates them to per-step logit masks. The DSL-first approach to constrained decoding; predates the FSM-index optimization.

new_papers.title: Finetuned Language Models Are Zero-Shot Learners (FLAN)
new_papers.authors: Wei, Bosma, Zhao, Guu, Yu, Lester, Du, Dai, Le
new_papers.summary: Original instruction-tuning paper. Reformats ~1.8K NLP tasks as natural-language instructions; SFT on the multi-task collection produces strong zero-shot generalization. The pre-RLHF post-training default.

new_papers.title: Scaling Instruction-Finetuned Language Models (Flan-T5)
new_papers.authors: Chung, Hou, Longpre, Zoph, Tay, Fedus, Li, Wang, Dehghani, Brahma, Webson, Gu, Dai, Suzgun, Chen, Chowdhery, Castro-Ros, Pellat, Robinson, Valter, Narang, Mishra, Yu, Zhao, Huang, Dai, Yu, Petrov, Chi, Dean, Devlin, Roberts, Zhou, Le, Wei
new_papers.summary: Scales FLAN to 1.8K tasks with multiple prompting techniques (zero-shot, few-shot, CoT). Establishes that adding CoT examples to instruction-tuning data improves reasoning generalization.
